target: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 1: reconcile 70 (dome-claimed) vs 72 (review catalog) drift across all prose — pick denominator convention and apply document-wide
decision_summary: Adopt Approach (a) — name the denominator at each mention — not Approach (b) (force all prose to 72). Rationale below. The fix has three parts: (1) a tiny build-pipeline change adding a {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} placeholder and replacing the hardcoded "70 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims" caption with computed values; (2) targeted prose patches at the 5 sites where a tally count (sum-of-72 numerator) is currently divided by a baseWins-length (70) denominator — those are arithmetically wrong; (3) one new explanatory paragraph near the Overview verdict bars ("Why 70 and 72 are both real numbers") so a reader who notices the drift gets the reconciliation in their first encounter, not buried in a caption.

kernel_of_truth.description: Both numbers are correct and load-bearing — neither is a bug to be erased. 70 is the dome's headline count of 'confirmed' WINs (currently exactly the entries in wins.json with three-digit IDs like '001'..'069'). 72 is our review catalog count, because the dome reused win-IDs 057 and 058 between its 'confirmed wins' list and its 'prospective predictions' list, and we split each collision into a parent row (057, 058) and a sub-claim row (057b, 058b) so each distinct claim gets its own verdict. Keeping both numbers in the prose carries an evidential point we do not want to lose: the dome has identifier-reuse problems (catalogued in Part 8b.1, Point 1 about claim_keys; that whole adoption section rests on the fact that the visible registry has duplicate WIN-NNN labels). If we collapse all prose to a single number, we either pretend the collisions don't exist (force 70) or pretend the dome itself claims 72 (force 72). Both options are worse than the current situation; the current situation is correctly two-numbered but inconsistently named.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The curmudgeon's grep is correct: 129 mentions of 70 vs 15 of 72 in the rendered HTML is integrity drift, and even though most '70' mentions are defensibly dome-frame, FIVE specific prose locations use {{TOTAL_WINS}} (=70) as a denominator for a verdict tally whose NUMERATOR is computed over all 72 catalog rows. Those five are arithmetically wrong — most visibly 'TALLY_STD=18 of TOTAL_WINS=70' (the 18 includes WIN-057b which is a collision sub-claim, so the math is 17/70 + 1 collision = 18/72, never 18/70). Two of the three categories whose tally count differs between the 70-frame and 72-frame are involved: Std Model Explains (18 catalog / 17 dome) because 057b lands there, and Not Demonstrated (3 catalog / 2 dome) because 058b lands there. Self-Contradicted, Refuted, Misleading, and Unfalsifiable happen to be the same in both frames at HEAD because neither collision row landed in those buckets — but the cleanest fix should be robust to a future verdict change on 057b/058b, not lucky-by-coincidence.

approach_a_vs_b_argument.chosen: (a) name the denominator at each mention
approach_a_vs_b_argument.rationale_for_a: The collision rows are evidence about the dome, not bookkeeping noise. WIN-057b ('Global cavity re-pressurization (SAA synchronous decay)') is a Std-Model-Explains finding for a dome claim that re-uses the WIN-057 number from 'Two-zone disc topology'; WIN-058b ('bedrock-κ') is a Not-Demonstrated finding for a dome claim that re-uses the WIN-058 number from 'θ=-lonE'. The fact that the dome reuses claim numbers across surfaces is a methodological point we make explicitly in Part 8b.1. If prose hides the 72-figure or hides the 70-figure, the structural argument that the visible registry has collisions loses one of its anchors.
approach_a_vs_b_argument.rationale_against_b: Force-72 would require rewording many sentences that are factually about what the dome itself claims ('the model claims 70 confirmed predictions', 'V51.0: 70 claimed wins') — those quotes and paraphrases are anchored to dome's own framing and reading '72' would misrepresent what the dome says. Force-72 would also break the V51.0 accuracy formula prose in §6.6, which is specifically about dome's own arithmetic on 70 with denominators of (70+4) and (70+4+silent_failures). Those are dome-side facts and cannot be re-templated to 72.
approach_a_vs_b_argument.why_curmudgeon_called_either_acceptable: Curmudgeon's c2 review wrote 'either path is fine' because their primary critique was integrity drift, not the choice of frame. The actual fix-set under (a) — the patches listed below — addresses exactly the same drift curmudgeon flagged, so the recommended action and curmudgeon's recommendation are compatible.

change_1.location: resolvePlaceholders function, ~line 61, in the replacements object

totalWins: counts.total,

change_3.current:
How the ${total} catalog entries actually land

${total} catalog entries = 70 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims. Sorted by count; percentages rounded to one decimal so they sum to 100. Click any verdict to jump to the detailed analysis.
change_3.replacement:
How the ${total} catalog entries actually land

${total} catalog entries = ${total - (context.collisionCount||0)} dome-claimed wins plus ${context.collisionCount||0} numbered-collision sub-claims. The dome reused win-IDs across its confirmed-wins and prospective-predictions surfaces; we split each collision into a parent row and a sub-claim row so every distinct dome claim gets its own verdict. Sorted by count; percentages rounded to one decimal so they sum to 100. Click any verdict to jump to the detailed analysis.
change_3.note: If the chart function does not currently receive `context` as a parameter, EITHER (a) pass it through from the call site (one extra arg) OR (b) hardcode the values as a temporary measure but mark the line with a // TODO: drive from context comment. Option (a) is preferred; option (b) is a 30-second fix if (a) requires plumbing.

add_keys.{{CATALOG_TOTAL}}: Total rows in wins.json (currently 72) — used as the denominator anywhere a verdict tally count is the numerator. Differs from {{TOTAL_WINS}} (the dome's claimed count, currently 70) because the catalog splits dome's two ID-collision claims into sub-rows.
add_keys.{{COLLISION_COUNT}}: Number of collision sub-claim rows (currently 2: WIN-057b, WIN-058b). Equals {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} − {{TOTAL_WINS}}.

prose_patches.replacement_html: Pattern 11 (NEW): Self-Contradicting Own Geometry. In {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} of the {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} catalog rows we reviewed, the dome's own stated geometry

prose_patches.location: Overview section — recommend inserting immediately AFTER the verdict-bars block (after the existing 'Sorted by count...' caption) and BEFORE the Latest Findings block, as a one-paragraph reading guide.
prose_patches.current_html: (no current text — this is an insertion, not a replacement)
prose_patches.replacement_html:
Two numbers, 70 and 72 — why both are real. The dome claims 70 confirmed wins. Our catalog has 72 rows. The 2-row difference is two ID collisions the dome creates by reusing WIN-057 and WIN-058 across its confirmed and prospective lists; we split each collision so every distinct claim gets its own verdict.

Throughout this review, count-phrasing uses two conventions: prose about what the dome claims uses 70 (the dome's own count of confirmed wins); prose about our review catalog uses 72 (because the catalog covers all 70 dome-claimed wins plus the 2 collision sub-claims, WIN-057b and WIN-058b, that the dome creates by reusing win-IDs across its confirmed and prospective surfaces). Verdict tallies (Self-Contradicted, Refuted, Misleading, etc.) are computed over all 72 catalog rows, so any sentence reading \"tally-count of denominator\" uses 72 as the denominator. Sentences quoting or paraphrasing the dome's own framing (\"V51.0 claims 70 wins\", \"the dome's 70-claim accuracy formula\") use 70. The collision-split itself is a structural finding about the dome's identifier hygiene; see Section 8b.1 (claim_key annotations) for the full discussion.
prose_patches.note: This is the single reader-facing reconciliation block — without it, even a reader who notices the convention has to reverse-engineer it. The progressive-disclosure wrapper keeps the Overview short; only a reader checking the math expands it. Cross-links to Section 8b.1 close the loop with the existing collision-as-evidence argument.

mentions_left_unchanged.rationale: Of the 44 {{TOTAL_WINS}} occurrences in sections.json, the 5 patched above (P1-P5) are the ones where the denominator semantics are arithmetically wrong (TALLY_* numerator over baseWins-length denominator). The remaining 39 are correctly dome-frame: either they quote/paraphrase the dome's own framing ('the model claims 70 confirmed predictions', 'V51.0: 70 claimed wins', 'the dome's 70-claim formula 70/(70+4)=95.2%'), or they are code-analysis denominators where the numerator (CA_*) is also computed over baseWins (code-analysis filters baseWins per generate-html.js line 1207, so 'CA_RELABELS of TOTAL_WINS' is internally consistent at 70/70). The audit pass below confirms each unchanged location.
sampled_unchanged_locations: part1@497 — Overview KPI card 'Claims Made: {{TOTAL_WINS}}', sublabel 'The dome's own count of confirmed predictions' — already dome-labelled.
part1@4504 — 'The model claims {{TOTAL_WINS}} confirmed predictions' — direct dome paraphrase.
part2b@560 — 'Of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs, {{CA_REVIEWED}} have been reviewed' — code-analysis denominator; reviewed=baseWins.filter(...), so internally consistent.
part2b@21379 — '{{TOTAL_WINS}} minus {{CA_DOME}} of {{TOTAL_WINS}} WINs work backwards' — code-analysis numerator over baseWins; consistent.
part6@37103 — 'The V51.0 formula {{TOTAL_WINS}} / ({{TOTAL_WINS}} + 4) = 95.2%' — quoting dome's own arithmetic; must stay 70.
part9@8741 — 'The {{TOTAL_WINS}} claimed wins include systematic duplication' — direct dome paraphrase.
part10@6028 — 'This review represents an analysis of {{TOTAL_WINS}} claimed wins' — footer, dome-frame is correct.
part1b@761 — 'V51.0 (April 2026): {{TOTAL_WINS}} claimed wins (+{{NEW_IN_V51}})' — version-history note, dome-frame.
(33 more, all dome-frame on the same audit principle — full list available on request)

current_values_at_authoring.arithmetic_bug_at_head: Pre-patch, the rendered prose says 'Tally_STD=18 of TOTAL_WINS=70' (Part 2b §2b.6, P1 location). 18 of 70 is misleading because the 18 includes WIN-057b which is not in the 70-set. Post-patch, the prose reads 'Tally_STD=18 of CATALOG_TOTAL=72' which matches the actual numerator/denominator pair.

holes_addressed: HOL-NARRATIVE c2 hole 1 (moderate): 70-vs-72 count drift across prose — reconciled via Approach (a), patches P1-P6.

test_recommendations.pre_commit_check: node test.js should be run by the decider before commit per CLAUDE.md's 'Critical Workflow Rule: Run Tests Before Committing Data Changes' — any HTML count drift will surface in the existing test suite.
test_recommendations.add_a_new_test: Recommend a tests/run.sh assertion: after rebuild, grep the rendered docs/index.html for '{{TALLY_STD}} of 70' and 'TALLY_STD of {{TOTAL_WINS}}' patterns (post-substitution: '18 of 70', '17 of 70' etc. paired with verdict-tally context). Fail the test if any tally-numerator/baseWins-denominator pair appears outside an explicitly-labelled dome-frame context. This codifies the editorial standard so it doesn't decay in the next prose edit. Implementation sketch: a small node script that re-counts the rendered HTML for the specific bad patterns and exits 1 if any are found.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why patch only 5 mentions when curmudgeon's grep counted 129 vs 15? Aren't you under-fixing the problem?
anticipated_objections.response: Curmudgeon counted post-substitution occurrences in the rendered HTML: the 44 {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholders in sections.json each render as '70', so they appear as 44 of the 129 '70' mentions; the rest are non-templated narrative paragraphs that quote the dome's '70 wins' framing directly. Of the 44 placeholder occurrences, only 5 are arithmetically wrong (tally-numerator over basewins-denominator). The other 39 are correct dome-frame uses — fixing them would mean replacing 'The model claims 70 predictions' with 'The model claims 72 predictions', which would misrepresent what the dome claims. The fix is targeted because the bug is targeted; the surface area is large but the actual bad-math is small.

anticipated_objections.objection: Approach (b) — force everything to 72 — is simpler. Why pay the complexity cost of two denominator placeholders?
anticipated_objections.response: Three reasons. (1) The dome itself claims 70; quoting or paraphrasing the dome with '72' is factually wrong. (2) The collision-split is itself evidence about the dome (it's the kernel-of-truth here) — collapsing it erases that evidence. (3) Sections 6.6, 8.2, 9.3, 8b.1 are specifically arguing about the dome's own counting, where the 70-figure is the load-bearing number. Force-72 would require reworking all of those sections and would still leave the same kind of mismatch in the other direction (TALLY counts split into 'visible vs hidden' counterparts to track which sub-claims are in the 72-frame). Two placeholders is the minimum complexity that makes the math correct without rewriting load-bearing argument sections.

anticipated_objections.objection: What if the dome adds a third collision (WIN-019b or similar) tomorrow? The placeholder math has to track that automatically.
anticipated_objections.response: It does, by construction. {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} = wins.length and {{COLLISION_COUNT}} = wins.length - baseWins.length. The build-time sanity check (build_pipeline_changes.change_2.build_invariant) explicitly fails if a row's id-shape isn't matched by either the baseWins regex (\d{3}) or the collisionWins regex (\d{3}[a-z]). So a future ID shape (e.g., '019c' or '042bb') will fail the build loudly until the regexes are updated. The caption in change_3 is also dynamic; it will read '72 catalog entries = 70 dome-claimed + 2 collisions' today and '73 = 70 + 3' tomorrow without further code changes.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why insert the explanatory paragraph P6 in the Overview rather than in §3.1 right above the verdict-tally header you also patched?
anticipated_objections.response: Overview placement is for the reader who skims the verdict bars in the hero and notices that bars total 72 while the surrounding KPI card says '70 Claims Made'. That reader's first question — 'why two numbers?' — needs an immediate answer, not a click-through to §3.1. §3.1's own header (patch P2) carries a one-line abbreviation of the same reconciliation ('72 catalog rows = 70 dome-claimed + 2 collision sub-claims'), so the reader who goes straight to Part 3 also gets the convention named. The full explanatory block exists once in the Overview; everywhere else, the abbreviation is sufficient and consistent.

anticipated_objections.objection: The curmudgeon's recommended-action was 'minor_edit', not a fix this large. Are you overshooting scope?
anticipated_objections.response: 'minor_edit' refers to the per-section impact, not the cumulative patch size. Each individual prose patch is one clause-level swap; the build-pipeline change is a few lines of code; the explanatory paragraph is one new <details> block. No section gets a structural rewrite. The cumulative count of edits is moderate because the bug is distributed across five sections, but each edit individually is small. This matches curmudgeon's recommended_action='minor_edit' framing.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also fix the related Part 2b derive-count template glitch (HOL-NARRATIVE hole 2) in this same EXP? They're conceptually related — both are template-vs-prose count drift.
anticipated_objections.response: Bundling would conflate two distinct root causes. Hole 1 is denominator-semantics drift (the {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholder is used where {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} is the correct denominator). Hole 2 is template-vs-prose drift on derives_from_dome=true (a singular/plural agreement issue plus a count-vs-list mismatch). A patch that addresses both would be harder to review; a future regression on one would be harder to roll back without reverting the other. Each gets its own EXP; this one is hole 1 only.

decider_routing_hints.integration_order: P1-P5 (prose patches in sections.json) can land in any order; they are independent. The build-pipeline change (changes 1-4) must land BEFORE the prose patches reference the new {{CATALOG_TOTAL}} placeholder, otherwise the rendered HTML will show the literal string '{{CATALOG_TOTAL}}'. Recommend a single decider commit that does all build changes + all prose patches atomically, then runs node build.js to verify and node test.js to test.
related_followups: EXP-441 (still pending): not directly related, but introduces a list_of_winids helper — if both land in the same cycle, the new {{COLLISION_COUNT}} computation can share file-organization conventions with the EXP-441 helper.
EXP-442 (still pending): not directly related; both are in part2b but touch different paragraphs.
